title: Bat Removal & Exclusion Services in New Brunswick, NJ | $900 - $2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in New Brunswick, NJ. Humane, effective solutions for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: New Brunswick residents facing bat infestations need fast, humane solutions that comply with New Jersey wildlife regulations. Our expert bat removal and exclusion services protect your home or business from these protected yet problematic mammals. We serve all New Brunswick neighborhoods with safe, effective bat control starting at $900.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over a decade of bat removal expertise to New Brunswick, combining humane exclusion methods with permanent sealing solutions. Our licensed technicians understand local bat species and New Jersey conservation laws, ensuring compliant removal and guaranteed results for your property.
localContext: New Brunswick's diverse architecture—from historic Victorian homes near Buccleuch Mansion to modern apartments downtown near Rutgers University—provides numerous entry points for bats seeking shelter. The city's proximity to the Raritan River and older building stock in neighborhoods like Feaster Park make professional bat exclusion essential for property owners.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in New Brunswick, NJ?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in New Brunswick typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on colony size, entry points, and building height. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before any work begins, with no hidden fees.

faqs.question: Is it legal to remove bats in New Brunswick?
faqs.answer: Yes, but New Jersey law requires humane exclusion methods and prohibits removal during maternity season (May through August). Our licensed technicians follow all state and local regulations, using one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my New Brunswick home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, grease marks around openings, and seeing bats enter or exit at twilight. We offer free inspections to confirm bat presence and identify entry points.

faqs.question: How long does bat exclusion take in New Brunswick?
faqs.answer: Most bat exclusion projects take 1-3 days for initial installation, with devices left in place for 3-7 days to ensure all bats exit. Complete sealing and cleanup typically occurs within two weeks, though timelines vary based on colony size and property complexity.
